description: Golden examples for answer-chain extraction quality. Numeric worked values may appear in model answers/checklists, but not reusable answerChain text.

cases.name: generic spring elastic energy chain is reusable

answerChain.title: Calculate elastic potential energy in a spring
answerChain.canonicalChainText: Convert the extension or compression into metres, use $E_e=\frac{1}{2}ke^2$, substitute the spring constant and extension or compression, calculate the energy, and give the unit.
answerChain.summary: Convert the extension or compression to metres, substitute into $E_e=\frac{1}{2}ke^2$, square the extension or compression, and give an energy unit.

steps.stepText: Convert the extension or compression into metres.
steps.explanation: The equation uses metres for extension or compression.
steps.commonOmission: Using the centimetre value directly as metres.

steps.explanation: This equation links elastic potential energy, spring constant and extension or compression.

steps.stepText: Substitute the spring constant and extension or compression into the equation.
steps.explanation: The extension or compression must be squared after substitution.
steps.commonOmission: Multiplying by the extension or compression instead of squaring it.

steps.explanation: This gives the energy stored elastically in the spring.
steps.commonOmission: Rounding too early or omitting the factor of one half.

steps.stepText: Give the unit as J or joule.
steps.commonOmission: Giving N/m or N as the unit.

cases.name: worked spring solution is rejected as a chain

answerChain.title: Calculate elastic potential energy in a spring
answerChain.canonicalChainText: Convert the extension or compression into metres, use $E_e=\frac{1}{2}ke^2$, substitute the spring constant and extension/compression, calculate the energy, and give the unit joule.
answerChain.summary: Convert cm to m, substitute into $E_e=\frac{1}{2}ke^2$, square the compression, and give J.

steps.explanation: The equation uses metres for extension or compression.
This equation links elastic potential energy, spring constant and extension or compression.
The compression must be squared after substitution.
This is the numerical elastic potential energy stored.
steps.commonOmission: Rounding too early or omitting the factor of one half.

cases.name: numeric model answer is allowed when chain is generic

answerChain.title: Calculate elastic potential energy in a spring
answerChain.canonicalChainText: Convert the extension or compression into metres, use $E_e=\frac{1}{2}ke^2$, substitute the spring constant and extension or compression, calculate the energy, and give the unit.
answerChain.summary: Use the elastic potential energy equation with consistent units.

steps.stepText: Convert the extension or compression into metres.
Calculate the energy and give an energy unit.

cases.name: known numeric recall is review-only, not blocking

answerChain.title: Recall UK mains potential difference
answerChain.canonicalChainText: Recall that UK mains electricity has a potential difference of 230 V.
answerChain.summary: Use the standard UK mains potential difference.

steps.stepText: State that UK mains potential difference is 230 V.
